evidence_quotes.quote: Companies just removing access to your Video game without any way to play them again.
Getting your purchased products taken away, because you run a software the Publisher doesn't like.
This is mostly about DRMs, like Denuvo, that take the access from the games you own, if you use it on too many different PCs in one day.
There are multiple issues I have with the video game industry.
Purchased Games that are unplayable for 24h if you use it on too many devices on one day.
Online connection needed for a game that would have no issues running offline.
On a lot of Operating systems, the companies are basically allowed to gather all your information without you being able to deactivate it.
They are also able to just ignore the choice you did before and activate whatever they want on your next boot or update.

paragraphs.text: Customer protection for games. There are multiple issues I have with the video game industry. 1. Companies just removing access to your Video game without any way to play them again. 2. Companies making a product unplayable without any compensation. I played a lot of GTA online and lost access to it last year, because they decided that my operating system wasn't allowed on the online service any more. I contacted them. I contacted Rockstar Games. Here are the two answers I got 7 Oct. 2024: "We do not have any information to provide on whether there will be a compensation or not, [...]" 8. Nov. 2024: "I am afraid that in your case, we will not be able to offer support and we do not offer any kind of compensation for this." This is the answer I got after they made the game I paid for unplayable and changed it to something else, and I still can't play it to this day. And that is something that happens regularly. 3. Invasive software that risks the integrity and privacy of the system. This is mostly a problem with Kernel level anti cheat, where anti cheat gets into the Kernel and one wrong line of code and the whole system breaks. It also allows seeing everything that is running on the system and everything that a person has on their Computer. This is the same problem that caused CrowdStrike, just in a different sector. 4. Purchased Games that are unplayable for 24h if you use it on too many devices on one day. This is mostly about DRMs, like Denuvo, that take the access from the games you own, if you use it on too many different PCs in one day. This gets even worse, if you are on Linux and use a translation layer, as switching the translation layer counts as a new PC. Games like Doom the dark ages and Monster Hunter Wilds use that, both use Denovu. This is also a big reason why I can no longer play Minecraft Bedrock edition and Sea of Thieves on my PC, since I switched to Linux. 5. Getting your purchased products taken away, because you run a software the Publisher doesn't like. This is mostly my own story. I got banned from Fortnite in 2018, this included my whole Epic games account to be banned. For context, I did buy other games on that account, I did spend money on Fortnite, and I didn't do anything that wasn't allowed in game, other than running a software Epic Games didn't want me to run. I don't remember the software, just that it didn't change anything in the game. I also contacted support at the time, and they told me "our software doesn't do mistakes". As an IT Student of the HTW Saar in Germany, I can easily say that this will never be true. 6. Online connection needed for a game that would have no issues running offline. This again is more a problem with DRM. 7. Loot box. There have been multiple researches that have shown that a Loot box mechanic in a game can cause a higher chance or gambling addiction later in life. This is especially problematic, because a lot of games that have a younger audience focus on that "mechanic". And now to privacy. On a lot of Operating systems, the companies are basically allowed to gather all your information without you being able to deactivate it. As an example, Google, Microsoft and Apple can know everything you do on your device, without you being able to deactivate it. And if you use an Android and get a custom ROM with less privacy concern, there is a good chance that stuff like contactless paying or your banking app won't work. So you kinda have no option. Either using Spyware or not being part of society. They are also able to just ignore the choice you did before and activate whatever they want on your next boot or update. I personally would like to see more support for open source alternatives, like OnlyOffice or LibreOffice, as they respect your privacy and choice. I know I just wrote a lot of things, and I know not all of them fixable. I just ask for them to be considered a problem and maybe some option for them to be handled better. Thank you for reading
